question: How many days before the team meeting I was preparing for did I attend the workshop on 'Effective Communication in the Workplace'?
ground_truth_answer: 7 days. 8 days (including the last day) is also acceptable.

retrieval_results.context:
User has attended a workshop titled 'Effective Communication in the Workplace' on January 10, which improved their active listening and conflict‑resolution skills, boosting confidence in presenting and engaging teams.
The user wants to understand why self-awareness is crucial in effective communication and how to continue developing this skill, reflecting on the workshop they attended.
User has made a personal note to practice active listening and conflict‑resolution skills during an upcoming team meeting on January 17, and wants suggestions on how to prioritize agenda items, allocate time for each discussion topic, and seek resources and advice on those topics to keep the meeting focused and efficient.
User is concerned about taking notes during the meeting and wants advice on effective note‑taking techniques and how to organize notes afterward, including examples of templates or formats to structure meeting minutes.
User likes the parking‑lot concept for non‑essential topics and wants guidance on handling situations where a team member raises an off‑agenda topic that still seems important, balancing agenda adherence with responsiveness.
The user wants tips on how to effectively convey complex information in a simple, clear, and concise way, focusing on making the message accessible to their audience.
User wants examples of digital note‑taking templates—such as Microsoft OneNote, Evernote, or Google Docs—to help them organize meeting notes efficiently.
The user is looking for tools or software to create visually appealing slides and diagrams for their presentation, and also wants recommendations for interactive elements such as polls or Q&A sessions.
